1.texto_original: Vuelve, oh Israel, a Jehová tu Dios; pues por tu pecado has caído.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Oseas 14:1

"Vuelve, oh Israel, a Jehová tu Dios; pues por tu pecado has caído."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Oseas 14:1

Philological Analysis

"Vuelve, oh Israel, a Jehová tu Dios; pues por tu pecado has caído."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Vuelve, oh Israel, a Jehová tu Dios; pues por tu pecado has caído."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

2.texto_original: Tomad con vosotros palabras, y volved a Jehová, y decidle: Quita toda iniquidad, y acéptanos con gracia, y daremos becerros de nuestros labios.

3.texto_original: No nos librará Asiria; no montaremos sobre caballos, ni nunca más diremos a la obra de nuestras manos: [Vosotros sois] nuestros dioses; porque en ti el huérfano alcanzará misericordia.
3.normal:
Devotional Study of Oseas 14:3

"No nos librará Asiria; no montaremos sobre caballos, ni nunca más diremos a la obra de nuestras manos: [Vosotros sois] nuestros dioses; porque en ti el huérfano alcanzará misericordia."

8.texto_original: Efraín [dirá]: ¿Qué más tendré ya con los ídolos? Yo lo oiré, y miraré; yo [seré] a él como el ciprés verde; de mí será hallado tu fruto.
8.normal:
Devotional Study of Oseas 14:8

"Efraín [dirá]: ¿Qué más tendré ya con los ídolos? Yo lo oiré, y miraré; yo [seré] a él como el ciprés verde; de mí será hallado tu fruto."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

9.texto_original: ¿Quién [es] sabio para que entienda esto, y prudente para que lo sepa? Porque los caminos de Jehová [son] rectos, y los justos andarán por ellos; mas los rebeldes tropezarán en ellos.
9.normal:
Devotional Study of Oseas 14:9

"¿Quién [es] sabio para que entienda esto, y prudente para que lo sepa? Porque los caminos de Jehová [son] rectos, y los justos andarán por ellos; mas los rebeldes tropezarán en ellos."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.
